popularStory.headline:
Henry Rice
(1620 – 10 February 1710)
popularStory.description:
d. Feb. 10 1710/11

m. Elizabeth Moore Feb. 1, 1643/4 - She d Aug. 3, 1705

Henry Rice was born in England and baptised at St. James' Church in Stanstead, Suffolk on February 13, 1620-21. 

His parent …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Rice於1620年2月13日出生在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德時，他的父親Deacon Edmund Rice27歲，母親Thomasine Frost19歲。他於1643年1月1日與Elizabeth Moore在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1711年2月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆享年90歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Rice于1620年2月13日出生在英国素福爾可斯坦斯亞德时，他的父亲Deacon Edmund Rice27岁，母亲Thomasine Frost19岁。他于1643年1月1日与Elizabeth Moore在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1711年2月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆享年90岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized pronunciation of one of the most common Welsh personal names, Rhys, from a form originally meaning ‘rash, impetuous’, also spelled Rys and Re(e)s. See also Reese , with which it is interchangeable as a result of different Anglicized forms of the Welsh vowel y, and also compare Preece and Price . Initial R- in Welsh is voiceless and often spelled Rh-, but in English R- is voiced as in the Anglicized surnames Rees and Rice. Welsh y is a short back vowel /ɪ/. In the medieval period the English approximation of this vowel was either /i/ or /e/, lengthened to /i:/ and /e:/. Subsequent sound changes in English produced the alternative pronunciations represented in Rees, Preece and Rice, Price. The name has also been established in Ireland from an early date.

English: either a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a thicket (Middle English ris, rice, ris, from Old English hrīs, Old Norse hrís), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with this word, such as Rise (East Yorkshire).

English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English Rys(e) and Re(e)s which when without a preposition could derive from one or other of several Old French and Middle English words, including Anglo-Norman French ris ‘laughter, smile’, Middle English ris, res ‘stem, stalk’, in origin the same word as in 2 above, and Middle English ris, rise, rice, res, Old French ris, riz ‘rice’, perhaps a nickname for a rice dealer or a cook.

variants.name: Reese
meanings: North German and Dutch (De Reese): nickname for a very big man, from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch rese ‘giant’.
German: habitational name from any of the places called Rees or Reese, in Rhineland and Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

variants.name: Ryce
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Rice  .

variants.name: Rise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Lief, Sven.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of over twenty farmsteads named Rise, from Old Norse hrís ‘brushwood’. The name also occurs in Sweden and Denmark.

variants.name: Wrice
meanings: Probably Welsh or English: variant of   Rice  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Rice was born on 13 February 1620, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England, his father, Deacon Edmund Rice, was 27 and his mother, Thomasine Frost, was 19. He married Elizabeth Moore on 1 January 1643, in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 10 February 1711, in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Rice est né le 13 février 1620, à Stanstead, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Deacon Edmund Rice, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Thomasine Frost, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Moore le 1 janvier 1643, à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé le 10 février 1711, à Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 90 ans.

Januar 1643 in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Elizabeth Moore geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb am 10. Februar 1711 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Rice è nato il il 13 febbraio 1620 nel Stanstead, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Deacon Edmund Rice, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Thomasine Frost, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Moore il 1 gennaio 1643, nel Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 10 febbraio 1711, nel Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 90 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Riceが1620年2月13日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドで生まれた時，父Deacon Edmund Riceは27歳，母Thomasine Frostは19歳であった。1643年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックススドブリーでElizabeth Mooreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1711年2月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスフラミンガムで90歳で死去した。

ko.Sudbury, Middlesex County, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이

ko.Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이
ko.lifeSummary: Henry Rice(이)가 1620년 2월 13일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Deacon Edmund Rice(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Thomasine Frost(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1643년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 Elizabeth Moore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1711년 2월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 프램잉함에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Rice nasceu em 13 de fevereiro de 1620, em Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Deacon Edmund Rice, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Thomasine Frost, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Moore em 1 de janeiro de 1643, em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de fevereiro de 1711, em Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 90 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Rice родился 13 февраля 1620 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Deacon Edmund Rice было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Thomasine Frost, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Moore 1 января 1643 г. в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 8 дочерей. Он умер 10 февраля 1711 г. в местности фрамингхам, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 90 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Rice nació el 13 de febrero de 1620, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Deacon Edmund Rice, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Thomasine Frost, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Moore el 1 de enero de 1643, en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 10 de febrero de 1711, en Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 90 años.
